selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 4.7% and 20-day momentum is 12.8%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 6.3% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.2%, downside volatility near 1.0%, and a recent max drawdown of 2.7%.
Volume confirmation is -31.8% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 2.4% versus SPY over 5 days and 1.0% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.82, confidence 0.73, and 0.6 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.82, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.6 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.75, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.73.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.197 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Bank of America Reports Growth in Demand for AI enabled Treasury and FX Tools Across APAC Region
news_evidence.llm_reason: Direct Bank of America update on increased demand for AI-enabled treasury and FX tools in APAC. Positive for fee income and strategic positioning, but likely incremental and not a near-term catalyst large enough to move the stock materially over 1–10 trading days.

news_evidence.title: S&P 500 Earnings: Can Banks Reignite Momentum as Reporting Season Begins?
news_evidence.llm_reason: Sector-level preview of banking earnings season that references BAC among peers. Useful for context on earnings expectations but does not contain new, BAC-specific financials or guidance that would be highly material within the next 1–10 trading days.
